FinnGenUtilsR 4.0.8

    • Tutorial connection to FinnGen BQ tables
    • Adding Names to Drug Events Data
    • Adding Names to Kanta Lab Data
    • Adding names to longitudinal and service sector codes
  • Changelog

Adding Names to Drug Events Data

tutorial_naming_drug_events_data.Rmd

Intro

This tutorial demonstrates how to work with drug events data using the FinnGenUtilsR package. The drug events data contains medication purchase and prescription records with VNR (Finnish national product codes) identifiers. This tutorial shows how to append human-readable medication names and ATC codes by joining with the finngen_vnrs reference table.

Unlike other data types (such as service sector or longitudinal data), there is no custom helper function for adding names to drug events data. Instead, we use a simple join operation with the finngen_vnrs table.

To understand what the fgbq object is, see the dedicated vignette tutorial_fgbq.

Set up 

library(dplyr)
#> 
#> Attaching package: 'dplyr'
#> The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':
#> 
#>     filter, lag
#> The following objects are masked from 'package:base':
#> 
#>     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union
library(FinnGenUtilsR)

Connect to the latest versions of BigQuery tables using get_fg_bq_tables(). Remember to change the environment to your sandbox, or set other data freeze versions if needed (see tutorial_fgbq vignette for more details).

fgbq <- get_fg_bq_tables(
  environment = "sandbox-XX"
)
#> Connecting to BigQuery...
#> Using data freeze: dev
#> Finding latest table versions...
#>   - birth_mother: sandbox_tools_dev.birth_mother_dev_dev
#>   - code_prevalence_stratified: sandbox_tools_dev.code_prevalence_stratified_dev_dev
#>   - covariates: sandbox_tools_dev.covariates_dev_dev
#>   - drug_events: sandbox_tools_dev.drug_events_dev_dev
#>   - endpoint_cohorts: sandbox_tools_dev.endpoint_cohorts_dev_dev
#>   - fg_codes_info: medical_codes.fg_codes_info_dev
#>   - finngen_vnrs: medical_codes.finngen_vnr_dev
#>   - hla_imputed: sandbox_tools_dev.hla_imputed_dev_dev
#>   - kanta: sandbox_tools_dev.kanta_dev_dev
#>   - kidney: sandbox_tools_dev.kidney_dev_dev
#>   - minimum_extended: sandbox_tools_dev.minimum_extended_dev_dev
#>   - plasma_samples: sandbox_tools_dev.plasma_samples_dev_dev
#>   - service_sector_detailed_longitudinal: sandbox_tools_dev.finngen_dev_service_sector_detailed_longitudinal_dev
#>   - spirometry: sandbox_tools_dev.spirometry_dev_dev
#>   - vaccination: sandbox_tools_dev.vaccination_dev_dev
#>   - vision: sandbox_tools_dev.vision_dev_dev
#>   - cdm_concept: finngen_omop_dev_dev.concept
#> Creating table connections (this may take a moment)...
#> Successfully connected to all 17 tables in 8.51 seconds

We can access the drug events data using the fgbq object as follows:

fgbq$tbl$drug_events |> 
  glimpse()
#> Rows: ??
#> Columns: 13
#> Database: BigQueryConnection
#> $ FINNGENID                     <chr> "FG00003640", "FG00001604", "FG00001940"…
#> $ PRESCRIPTION_ID               <chr> "8SI4VSTY13WLO56C4Z8I0ISXU15GQK", "CW8L9…
#> $ PRESCRIPTION_APPROX_EVENT_DAY <date> 2008-11-11, 1996-10-13, 2009-08-16, 200…
#> $ PRESCRIPTION_AGE              <dbl> 62.587, 53.856, 28.118, 51.420, 27.800, …
#> $ PRESCRIPTION_ATC              <chr> "M03BX02", "C10AA01", "C10AA01", "J01CE0…
#> $ PRESCRIPTION_VNR              <chr> "000976", "003664", "003678", "006826", …
#> $ MEDICATION_APPROX_EVENT_DAY   <date> 2008-11-11, 1996-10-13, 2009-08-16, 200…
#> $ MEDICATION_AGE                <dbl> 62.587, 53.856, 28.118, 51.420, 27.800, …
#> $ MEDICATION_ATC                <chr> "M03BX02", "C10AA01", "C10AA01", "J01CE0…
#> $ MEDICATION_VNR                <chr> "000976", "003664", "003678", "006826", …
#> $ MEDICATION_QUANTITY           <dbl> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, …
#> $ MERGED_SOURCE                 <chr> "PRESCRIPTION_DELIVERY", "PRESCRIPTION_D…
#> $ INDEX                         <chr> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, …

We can also view the finngen_vnrs reference table that contains medication names and ATC codes:

fgbq$tbl$finngen_vnrs |> 
  glimpse()
#> Rows: ??
#> Columns: 31
#> Database: BigQueryConnection
#> $ VNR                              <int64> 3, 71, 83, 626, 1247, 1248, 1447, 2…
#> $ ATC                              <chr> "J01DB05", "C10AA01", "A10BB07", "N01…
#> $ MedicineName                     <chr> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, N…
#> $ AdministrationRouteSourceTextFI  <chr> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, N…
#> $ AdministrationRoute              <chr> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, N…
#> $ DosageFormSourceTextFI           <chr> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, N…
#> $ DosageForm                       <chr> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, N…
#> $ PackageSourceTextFI              <chr> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, N…
#> $ PackageSize                      <dbl> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, N…
#> $ PackageFactor                    <int64> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA,…
#> $ PackageUnit                      <chr> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, N…
#> $ SubstanceSourceTextFI            <chr> "cefadroxil", "simvastatin", "glipizi…
#> $ Substance                        <chr> "cefadroxil", "simvastatin", "glipizi…
#> $ SubstanceStrengthTextFI          <chr> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, N…
#> $ Strength                         <chr> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, N…
#> $ SubstanceStrengthNumenatorValue  <dbl> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, N…
#> $ SubstanceStrengthNumenatorUnit   <chr> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, N…
#> $ SubstanceStrengthDeominatorValue <dbl> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, N…
#> $ SubstanceStrengthDeominatorUnit  <chr> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, N…
#> $ ValidRange                       <lgl> TRUE, TRUE, TRUE, TRUE, TRUE, TRUE, T…
#> $ Source                           <chr> "originalVNR", "originalVNR", "origin…
#> $ Status                           <chr> "incomplete_substance; incomplete_pac…
#> $ VNRnew                           <chr> "000003", "000071", "000083", "000626…
#> $ DDDPerPack                       <dbl> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, N…
#> $ calculateTotalStrength_message   <chr> "One input is NA", "One input is NA",…
#> $ TotalStrength                    <dbl> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, N…
#> $ TotalStrengthUnit                <chr> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, N…
#> $ n_codes                          <int64> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA,…
#> $ Dosage                           <dbl> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, N…
#> $ DosageUnit                       <chr> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, N…
#> $ MedicineNameFull                 <chr> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, N…

Generate a table for analysis

For example, we create a subset of the drug events table by querying events from subject “FG00003640”:

drug_events_subject_tbl <- fgbq$tbl$drug_events |> 
  filter(FINNGENID == "FG00003640") |> 
  arrange(desc(MEDICATION_APPROX_EVENT_DAY))

We can see that this table contains VNR codes (MEDICATION_VNR and PRESCRIPTION_VNR) but not the medication names:

drug_events_subject_tbl |> 
  glimpse()
#> Rows: ??
#> Columns: 13
#> Database: BigQueryConnection
#> Ordered by: desc(MEDICATION_APPROX_EVENT_DAY)
#> $ FINNGENID                     <chr> "FG00003640", "FG00003640", "FG00003640"…
#> $ PRESCRIPTION_ID               <chr> "VOHA7EIA9V8HZEDJ7UXJWFHDXSNS3D", "13ZS3…
#> $ PRESCRIPTION_APPROX_EVENT_DAY <date> 2022-12-30, 2022-12-28, 2022-12-27, 202…
#> $ PRESCRIPTION_AGE              <dbl> 76.720, 76.715, 76.712, 76.660, NA, NA, …
#> $ PRESCRIPTION_ATC              <chr> "G04AA01", "C08CA01", "S01ED51", "N05BA0…
#> $ PRESCRIPTION_VNR              <chr> "124339", "086625", "005064", "383968", …
#> $ MEDICATION_APPROX_EVENT_DAY   <date> 2022-12-30, 2022-12-28, 2022-12-27, 202…
#> $ MEDICATION_AGE                <dbl> 76.720, 76.715, 76.712, 76.660, 64.742, …
#> $ MEDICATION_ATC                <chr> "G04AA01", "C08CA01", "S01ED51", "N05BA0…
#> $ MEDICATION_VNR                <chr> "124339", "086625", "005064", "383968", …
#> $ MEDICATION_QUANTITY           <dbl> NA, NA, NA, NA, 1, 1, NA, 1, 2, 1, 1, 1,…
#> $ MERGED_SOURCE                 <chr> "PRESCRIPTION_DELIVERY", "PRESCRIPTION_D…
#> $ INDEX                         <chr> NA, NA, NA, NA, "1620854", "1620868", NA…

Adding medication information via VNR codes

To add medication names and ATC codes to the drug events data, we simply join with the finngen_vnrs reference table. The drug events table contains two VNR columns:

  • MEDICATION_VNR: The VNR code for the actual medication dispensed
  • PRESCRIPTION_VNR: The VNR code from the original prescription (may differ from what was dispensed)

We can join on either column depending on which medication information we need. Here we’ll join on MEDICATION_VNR:

Note: The VNR codes in the drug_events table are stored as strings, while in the finngen_vnrs table they are integers. We need to convert the type before joining.

drug_events_with_names <- drug_events_subject_tbl |> 
  mutate(MEDICATION_VNR = as.integer(MEDICATION_VNR)) |> 
  left_join(
    fgbq$tbl$finngen_vnrs,
    by = c("MEDICATION_VNR" = "VNR")
  )

Now we can see the medication names, ATC codes, and other information:

drug_events_with_names |> 
  dplyr::glimpse()
#> Rows: ??
#> Warning: ORDER BY is ignored in subqueries without LIMIT
#> ℹ Do you need to move arrange() later in the pipeline or use window_order() instead?
#> Columns: 43
#> Database: BigQueryConnection
#> Ordered by: desc(MEDICATION_APPROX_EVENT_DAY)
#> $ FINNGENID                        <chr> "FG00003640", "FG00003640", "FG000036…
#> $ PRESCRIPTION_ID                  <chr> "Z8RBL4B59IY4VWM0DKT31IDY6OQOFG", NA,…
#> $ PRESCRIPTION_APPROX_EVENT_DAY    <date> 2017-09-25, NA, 2019-07-13, 2019-07-…
#> $ PRESCRIPTION_AGE                 <dbl> 71.458, NA, 73.254, 73.254, 66.127, 5…
#> $ PRESCRIPTION_ATC                 <chr> "R01AD09", NA, "C09DA03", "C09DA03", …
#> $ PRESCRIPTION_VNR                 <chr> "474551", NA, "013767", "013767", "02…
#> $ MEDICATION_APPROX_EVENT_DAY      <date> 2017-09-25, 2019-02-03, 2019-07-13, …
#> $ MEDICATION_AGE                   <dbl> 71.458, 60.966, 73.254, 73.254, 66.12…
#> $ MEDICATION_ATC                   <chr> "R01AD09", "M01AE01", "C09DA03", "C09…
#> $ MEDICATION_VNR                   <int64> 474551, 434993, 13767, 13767, 21967…
#> $ MEDICATION_QUANTITY              <dbl> NA, 1, NA, NA, NA, NA, 1, NA, NA, NA,…
#> $ MERGED_SOURCE                    <chr> "PRESCRIPTION_DELIVERY", "KELA", "PRE…
#> $ INDEX                            <chr> NA, "1620793", NA, NA, NA, NA, "16208…
#> $ ATC                              <chr> "R01AD09", "M01AE01", "C09DA03", "C09…
#> $ MedicineName                     <chr> "NASONEX", "BURANA", "DIOVAN COMP", "…
#> $ AdministrationRouteSourceTextFI  <chr> "Nenään", "Suun kautta", "Suun kautta…
#> $ AdministrationRoute              <chr> "Nasal use", "Oral use", "Oral use", …
#> $ DosageFormSourceTextFI           <chr> "nenäsumute, suspensio", "TABLETTI, K…
#> $ DosageForm                       <chr> "nasal spray, suspension", "film-coat…
#> $ PackageSourceTextFI              <chr> "140 annosta", "100", "98 fol", "98 f…
#> $ PackageSize                      <dbl> 140, 100, 98, 98, 28, 100, 100, 28, 2…
#> $ PackageFactor                    <int64> 1, 1, 1, 1, 1, 1, 1, 3, 1, 1, 1, 1,…
#> $ PackageUnit                      <chr> "doses", "1", "fol", "fol", "fol", "p…
#> $ SubstanceSourceTextFI            <chr> "mometasonifuroaatti monohydraatti", …
#> $ Substance                        <chr> "mometasone furoate monohydrate", "ib…
#> $ SubstanceStrengthTextFI          <chr> "50 mikrog/annos", "800 mg", "160/25 …
#> $ Strength                         <chr> "50 mikrog/annos", "800 mg", "160 mg"…
#> $ SubstanceStrengthNumenatorValue  <dbl> 50.0, 800.0, 160.0, 25.0, 60.0, 100.0…
#> $ SubstanceStrengthNumenatorUnit   <chr> "ug", "mg", "mg", "mg", "mg", "ug", "…
#> $ SubstanceStrengthDeominatorValue <dbl> 1, 1, 1, 1, 1, 1, 1, NA, 1, 1, 1, 1, …
#> $ SubstanceStrengthDeominatorUnit  <chr> "doses", "1", "1", "1", "1", "1", "1"…
#> $ ValidRange                       <lgl> TRUE, TRUE, TRUE, TRUE, TRUE, TRUE, T…
#> $ Source                           <chr> "ltkVNR", "originalVNR", "ltkVNR", "l…
#> $ Status                           <chr> "complete", "complete", "complete", "…
#> $ VNRnew                           <chr> "474551", "434993", "013767", "013767…
#> $ DDDPerPack                       <dbl> 35.00000, 66.66000, 98.00000, 98.0000…
#> $ calculateTotalStrength_message   <chr> "correct", "correct", "correct", "cor…
#> $ TotalStrength                    <dbl> 7000, 80000, 15680, 2450, 1680, 10000…
#> $ TotalStrengthUnit                <chr> "ug", "mg", "mg", "mg", "mg", "ug", "…
#> $ n_codes                          <int64> 199204, 118807, 16721, 16721, 43816…
#> $ Dosage                           <dbl> 50.0, 800.0, 160.0, 25.0, 60.0, 100.0…
#> $ DosageUnit                       <chr> "ug/doses", "mg", "mg", "mg", "mg", "…
#> $ MedicineNameFull                 <chr> "NASONEX nasal spray, suspension 50 m…

Alternative: Running SQL Queries

You can also run SQL queries directly on the BigQuery tables. See the tutorial_service_sector_data vignette for examples of how to do this with the service sector data, but the same principles apply to the drug events data.

On this page

Developed by Javier Gracia-Tabuenca.
